audit.name: blind-list-scoped-get-not
assumptions: Blind run: the auditor received only the repository path and the A-SEC-3 definition.

findings.title: getInvoice resolves an invoice by request-supplied id with no tenant predicate (cross-tenant IDOR)
findings.impact: The handler loads an invoice using only the client-controlled `req.params.invoiceId` and returns the full record via `res.json(invoice)`, with no binding to `req.user.tenantId`. Any authenticated user who guesses or enumerates an invoice id reads another tenant's invoice in full. This is financial and cross-tenant data (billing amounts, counterparty identity, and any PII carried on the record), so the exposure is a direct confidentiality breach across the tenant boundary. The gap is made unambiguous by its siblings in the same file: `listInvoices` (line 5) filters on `{ tenantId: req.user.tenantId }` and `updateInvoice` (line 16) binds both `{ id, tenantId }`, so the tenant is available on the request and the omission on the read path is the sole deviation from the file's own pattern. There is no compensating control: no router-level ownership middleware, no post-load ownership assertion before `res.json`, and no row-level-security policy or migration anywhere in the repository (`src/db.js` is an in-memory stub with no tenant filtering of its own). The read is also the most enumerable path, since GET requires no body and no CSRF-relevant state.
findings.fix: Bind the id to the caller's tenant inside the query, matching the predicate already used by `updateInvoice`. Replace the lookup with a scoped `findOne`, e.g. `const invoice = await db.invoices.findOne({ id: req.params.invoiceId, tenantId: req.user.tenantId });`, keeping the existing `if (!invoice) return res.status(404).end();` so a cross-tenant id is indistinguishable from a missing one and enumeration leaks nothing. Add a `findOne` to the `db.invoices` stub in `src/db.js` to match. Rather than fixing this one call site, prefer making the scoping structural: expose a single `scopedInvoices(req)` accessor (or an equivalent repository bound to `req.user.tenantId`) that every handler must route through, and forbid direct `db.invoices.*` access from route code, so an unscoped `findById` cannot be reintroduced. A row-level-security policy on the invoices table keyed to the session tenant would enforce the same invariant at the database layer and is the strongest deny-by-default option once a real datastore replaces the stub. Add a regression test asserting that a GET for an invoice belonging to another tenant returns 404.

tasks.title: Bind the query to the caller: getInvoice resolves an invoice by request-supplied id with no tenant predicate (cross-tena
tasks.reuses: the scoping already applied by the sibling handlers
acceptance: The load binds the owner or tenant predicate inside the query.
A cross-tenant id returns 404 rather than the record.

overall.grade_scope: an arithmetic roll-up of model-assigned pass/fail from a source read, not a test result, scan, or certification
